channel.title: AORMS Blog
channel.description: Platform notes and AORMS-Studio practice operations for Indian architecture consultancies. AORMS is web-only; EOMS knowledge bank + ESTI internal AI. AORMS-Consultancy is live on the same spine.

item.title: AORMS-Consultancy — engineering on the same operational spine
item.description: AORMS-Consultancy is built for structural, MEP, and multidisciplinary engineering consultancies on the same AORMS spine as Studio — live for engineering consultancies on the same spine as Studio.

item.title: AORMS is web-only — no desktop apps, one browser workspace
item.description: AORMS-Studio and AORMS-Consultancy run in the browser. There is no Lite, Pro, or Manager desktop installer — one standard licence, storage and AI usage, sign in at studio.aorms.in.

item.title: Fee stages to Studio invoices — closing the billable path
item.description: Consultancy engagements carry fee stages that connect to AORMS-Studio invoices — so delivered advisory work stops sitting unbilled between two systems.

item.title: One Standard licence — storage and AI, not product tiers
item.description: AORMS licences the company on a Standard plan — usage is storage and AI, not Lite vs Pro desktop SKUs. Web workspaces only; BYO AI keys remain supported.

item.title: Pre-construction risks and opportunities — Brief to issue readiness
item.description: Before drawings issue, AORMS-Studio keeps a structured R&O register — risks and opportunities scored from Brief through CONCEPT to ISSUE_READINESS, tied to fee and reliance.

item.title: AORMS platform ? operational and design frameworks for AEC consultancies
item.description: AORMS is not solution delivery or construction project management. It gives AEC consulting firms an operational framework and a design framework on one spine, with EOMS as the knowledge bank and ESTI as the internal agent.

item.title: From meeting minutes to site-safe revisions — ESTI drafts, architects assess, clients approve
item.description: Meeting happens, minutes are issued, ESTI extracts revision drafts — but before site or drawings move, the architect marks criticality and the client approves the implications. The full gate, not just the draft.

item.title: Introducing AORMS — fee recovery and revision control for the Indian architecture practice
item.description: AORMS-Studio connects what a practice has delivered with what it has billed, and gives every client-driven revision a dated, approved record before site or drawings move — the first workspace on the AORMS platform.

item.title: Why we built AORMS — the story behind the practice management system
item.description: Brilliant design work held together by spreadsheets, messaging threads, and one senior person's memory — and fees leaking away before invoices were raised. The AORMS-Studio was built to close that gap on the AORMS platform.

item.title: Architecture practice management — the missing system
item.description: Practices run sophisticated project work on a fragmented office record — fees, drawings, revisions, and site held in separate tools. The missing spine is the one that connects commercial, delivery, and fee recovery for each project.

item.title: Best software for managing client approvals in architecture firms
item.description: Client approvals protect fee, liability, and programme — but only when the issued drawing, the approval evidence, and the fee record live on one durable trace. What to require from approval workflow software in Indian practice.

item.title: The real cost of poor project visibility in architecture studios
item.description: Poor visibility is not missing dashboards — it is not knowing fee exposure, approval blockers, and workload until they hurt. The cost is margin, trust, and principal burnout.

item.title: How better revision tracking prevents scope creep in architecture projects
item.description: Scope creep is cumulative — individually reasonable requests that are never logged. Revision tracking makes the total visible while there is still fee to recover and a conversation worth having.

item.title: Managing multiple client feedback loops efficiently
item.description: When every client channels feedback through a different medium, architects become human routers. One project record and scoped portals collapse the loops without killing responsiveness.

item.title: Why architecture teams miss early project warning signs
item.description: Late surprises are usually visible weeks earlier — in tasks, revisions, fees, and site notes. Studios miss them because signals live in different inboxes.

item.title: Why managing architecture projects on Excel eventually breaks down
item.description: Spreadsheets work until they don't — when drawings, fees, revisions, and site data outgrow rows and tabs. Here is where Excel fails architecture offices and what replaces it.

item.title: The architecture practice KPIs that actually matter
item.description: Six numbers a small or mid-size Indian architecture practice should track — fee realisation, revision mix, WIP, utilisation, approval turnaround and a fair team score.

item.title: The architecture project lifecycle, stage by stage
item.description: A stage-by-stage walk-through of an Indian architecture project — enquiry to handover — mapped to COA stages, with the documents and decisions each stage produces.

item.title: GST for architecture firms in India: a practical guide
item.description: SAC codes, 18% rate, place of supply, TDS net receivable, ITC, and how clean invoicing at every COA stage supports fee recovery — a plain-English guide for Indian practices.

item.title: Fee recovery for architecture practices: five places your billing goes missing
item.description: Earned fees slip away before an invoice is raised — staged billing gaps, unlogged client changes, approval delays, and more. Five leakage points in a design practice, and how a live record closes each.

item.title: Managing consultant coordination on architecture projects
item.description: A practical guide to coordinating structural, MEP and PHE consultants — RFIs, transmittals and version control that keep one coordinated set the site can build from.

item.title: How to structure and recover fees for architectural services in India
item.description: COA stage fees, percentage vs lump-sum vs area-based pricing, stage-wise invoicing, and protecting fee recovery when clients drive revisions — a working guide for Indian practices.

item.title: Why architecture firms lose projects due to poor client communication
item.description: Most lost architecture projects aren't lost on design — they're lost in slow, scattered, undocumented client communication. A deep look at where it breaks, what it costs, and how to fix it for good.

item.title: Why our dashboard is calm — and not a military or Bloomberg terminal
item.description: Most dashboards optimise for density. Ours optimises for attention. Here is the cognitive science behind a deliberately quiet screen.

item.title: How to track design revisions without workflow chaos
item.description: Revisions are the work, not a failure — but tracking them in file names, email threads, and memory loses fees and causes disputes. A complete, practical system for tracking every design revision cleanly, for studios of any size.

item.title: Predicting your next task — the priority and reasoning engine
item.description: How AORMS ranks every open task into one recommended action, and how it still reasons usefully when the dependency data is incomplete.

item.title: The hidden cost of untracked client revisions in architecture projects
item.description: Untracked client revisions silently erode fee recovery — each absorbed request blends into the project until there is nothing left to bill. A clear look at the real cost, and how to make it visible.

item.title: GST invoicing for Indian architecture practices, done right
item.description: SAC codes, CGST/SGST vs IGST, TDS net receivable, and composition levy — the details that keep fee recovery on track and your filing clean at every stage.

item.title: Why generic project management tools fail architecture firms
item.description: Generic task boards and trackers were not built for drawings, revisions, COA fees, GST, approvals, or site records. Why generic tools never fit an architecture consultancy — and what does.

item.title: Revision intelligence — turning client-driven changes into billable data
item.description: Not all revisions are equal. AORMS classifies every change by category and source, so client scope creep, fee implications, and internal errors stop hiding in the same number.

item.title: How to build an efficient drawing approval workflow for architecture projects
item.description: Most delivery delays in architecture aren't drawing time — they're approval time. A complete, practical guide to designing a drawing approval workflow that keeps projects moving.

item.title: Measuring team performance fairly — the ASPRF score
item.description: Counting finished tasks rewards the wrong behaviour. ASPRF weighs reliability, quality, impact, collaboration, learning, and wellbeing — with anti-gaming built in.

item.title: Smart access — team hierarchy and who can see what
item.description: Roles, capabilities, staff levels, teams, and scoped portals. Everyone sees exactly what their work needs — and nothing it doesn't.

item.title: Try AORMS — the live demo and how to explore it
item.description: A fully seeded AORMS-Studio practice on the public demo site — projects, billing, a team, client portals — sign in with the demo principal account.
